title: wrightia zeylanica
intro: Wrightia zeylanica, also known as Jaya or Malay Wrightia, is a deciduous tree native to Sri Lanka and parts of Southeast Asia, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits associated with Wrightia zeylanica include its ability to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and improve digestion. The plant can be used in various herbal preparations, such as decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which can be applied topically to the skin or consumed orally after proper preparation and consultation with a healthcare professional.
